Resolves #15261

Breaks the §5.2-trigger-list vs §6.1-blast-class name collision in the ideation-sandbox workflow with two trigger-scoped clarifying sentences — one at each reading entry point. An author reading §6.1 first now learns the blast-class governs the Consensus Mandate only and §5.2's triggers fire independently; a reviewer reading §5.2's out-of-scope paragraph first now sees its exemption is narrower than §6.1's low-blast class, with the live D#15249 misread as the empirical anchor. No gate semantics change.

Evidence: L2 achieved (docs-only substrate delta; both seams verified against the live file) → L2 required (the close-target ACs name text placement and semantic neutrality; no runtime surface exists). Residual: none.

## Substrate-mutation slot-rationale (§1.1)

- **Modified section §5.2 "Out of scope" — disposition: rewrite (clarify).** Trigger-frequency: every graduation classification (multiple per week in the current cadence). Failure-severity: demonstrated same-day two-maintainer misread costing a full `[GRADUATION_DEFERRED]` cycle and risking premature comment-as-authority. Enforceability: text-level, self-enforcing at read time — the sentence sits exactly where the misread happened.
- **Modified section §6.1 classification paragraph — disposition: rewrite (clarify).** Same rationale from the author-side entry point.
- Net delta: +2 sentences. Decay mitigation: cross-reference clarifications, not new gates; sunset condition — if the two classification axes are ever merged into one (a legitimate future MX-loop outcome), both sentences retire with the merge.

## Deltas from ticket

- None substantive. Both sentences landed at the ticketed seams; the §5.2 sentence additionally renames the exemption's subject ("proposals outside every trigger above") to break the name collision at its root, exactly as the ticket's Fix prescribed.
- Lint-driven compression (cycle 0, pre-review): the skill-manifest lint enforces the accretion defense mechanically — the file sat exactly AT its 25,000-byte budget, so the clarification had to pay for itself. Both sentences compressed + three lossless dedups (a subsumed duplicate empirical anchor, a self-restating trigger clause, a duplicated epistemics sentence). **Net vs dev: −61 bytes** with both entry-point clarifications intact — the sunset rationale upgraded to net-reduction.
- Pre-review catch (cross-family, no formal cycle spent): the unchanged §6.1 low-blast table CELL still said "§5.1 … suffices" — false for the epic-bound class, and a table misread that prose cannot retire. Cell now states the scope split (§5.1 for §6 consensus only; §5.2 fires independently). Net vs dev after the cell fix: **−36 bytes**, still under the payload budget.

## /turn-memory-pre-flight receipt (substrate-mutation, §1.1 complement)

Step 2 routing: the mutated file is the ideation-sandbox skill's World-Atlas reference payload — conditionally loaded via the existing SKILL.md router only when the skill fires; it creates no duplicate turn-load and no new always-loaded bytes (reviewer mechanically verified the Codex-side load path loads CODEX.md only, no `context.fileName` duplicate; same conditional-load shape on the Claude side). Payload budget enforced mechanically by `lint-skill-manifest` (OK at head; net negative vs dev).

## Test Evidence

- Docs-only substrate delta: no runtime or spec surface. `.agents/skills/**` spec coverage: `None found` (by design — payloads are prose substrate).
- Seam verification: both replaced strings matched uniquely against the live file (anchored edits); the D#15249 anchor cited in-text follows the file's own empirical-anchor house style (`#11180`, `#10697`).

## Post-Merge Validation

- [ ] The next epic-bound Discussion classification correctly separates the two axes (the D#15249 misread class does not recur).

## Related

- D#15249 (the live misread), PR #15255 cycles 1–2 (the catch + conservative acceptance), #15254.
